zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard Farley於1531年5月29日出生在英國素瑞吉爾德福德時，他的父親John Alfred Farley24歲，母親Agnes Morthdon12歲。他於1554年與Jane Arrow在英國英格蘭素瑞阿爾布瑞結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1561年9月26日在英國英格蘭素瑞阿爾布瑞享年30歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭薩默塞特邦藍 聖 瑪麗。

nameMeanings.name: Farley
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of various places called Farley, of which there are examples in Berkshire, Derbyshire, Hampshire, Kent, Somerset, Gloucestershire, Staffordshire, Surrey, Wiltshire, Shropshire, and Sussex. From Old English fearn ‘fern’ + lēah ‘woodland clearing’. See also Farleigh , Fairley , Fairlie .
Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Fearghaile (see Farrelly ).

variants.name: Farleigh
meanings: English: habitational name from any of various places called Farleigh, of which there are examples in Hampshire, Devon, Kent, Somerset, Surrey, and Wiltshire. From Old English fearn ‘fern’ + lēah ‘woodland clearing’. See also Farley , Fairley , Fairlie .

variants.name: Farlow
meanings: English: habitational name from a place in Shropshire, so named from Old English fearn ‘fern’ + hlāw ‘hill, tumulus’. This surname may have become confused with Farley .

variants.name: Fearnley
meanings: English (Yorkshire): from any of various places called with Old English fearn ‘fern, bracken’ + lēah ‘wood, clearing’, notably the Yorkshire places Farnley near Otley, Farnley near Armley, Farnley Tyas, Fearnley in Wyke, Fernley Hill in Altofts, and perhaps also Fernilee in Derbyshire.

variants.name: Farlee
meanings: English and Irish: variant of   Farley  .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

en.lifeSummary: When Richard Farley was born on 29 May 1531, in Guildford, Surrey, England, his father, John Alfred Farley, was 24 and his mother, Agnes Morthdon, was 12. He married Jane Arrow in 1554, in Albury, Surrey, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died on 26 September 1561, in Albury, Surrey, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 30, and was buried in Buckland St Mary, Somerset, England, United Kingdom.

Il a épousé Jane Arrow en 1554, à Albury, Surrey, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé le 26 septembre 1561, à Albury, Surrey, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 30 ans, et a été enterré à Buckland St Mary, Somerset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

September 1561 im Alter von 30 Jahren in Albury, Surrey, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Buckland St Mary, Somerset, England, United Kingdom begraben.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard Farleyが1531年5月29日にイギリススリーグイルドフォードで生まれた時，父John Alfred Farleyは24歳，母Agnes Morthdonは12歳であった。1554年にイギリスイギリススリーアルブリーでJane Arrowと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼は1561年9月26日にイギリスイギリススリーアルブリーで30歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスソメルセトブックランド スト マリーにて埋葬された。

ko.Buckland Saint Mary, Chard, Somerset, En: 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트 벅랜드 스트 메리
ko.lifeSummary: Richard Farley(이)가 1531년 5월 29일 영국 수리 길드퍼드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Alfred Farley(은)는 24세였고, 그의 어머니 Agnes Morthdon(은)는 12세였다. 그는 1554년 영국 잉글랜드 수리 알버이에서 Jane Arrow(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1561년 9월 26일 영국 잉글랜드 수리 알버이에서 30세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트 벅랜드 스트 메리에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Farley nasceu em 29 de maio de 1531, em Guildford, Surrey, Inglaterra, seu pai, John Alfred Farley, tinha 24 anos e sua mãe, Agnes Morthdon, tinha 12 anos. Ele casou-se com Jane Arrow em 1554, em Albury, Surrey, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. ru.Buckland Saint Mary, Chard, Somerset, En: буккланд ст мэри, сомирсет, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard Farley родился 29 мая 1531 г. в местности гуаилдфорд, сурри, Англия, его отцу по имени John Alfred Farley было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Agnes Morthdon, 12. Он женился на женщине по имени Jane Arrow в 1554 г. в местности албури, сурри, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер 26 сентября 1561 г. в местности албури, сурри, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 30 лет и был похоронен в местности буккланд ст мэри, сомирсет, Англия, Великобритания.
